meta_title: For Students: SaaS pricing to Solve no portfolio (Intermediate Comparison)
meta_description: Learn how to SaaS pricing and overcome no portfolio. A Intermediate-level Comparison using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: SaaS Pricing to Solve No Portfolio
content.seo_description: Learn to implement SaaS pricing models to overcome your lack of a portfolio. An intermediate guide using Antigravity and Claude Code for AI builders.
content.answer_block: To overcome a lack of portfolio, build a functional SaaS pricing engine. By implementing tiered subscription logic, Stripe API integration, and dynamic usage-based billing, you demonstrate high-value engineering skills. This transforms your GitHub from a collection of tutorials into a professional-grade product that proves your ability to handle real-world revenue logic.
content.intro:
You stare at a blank GitHub profile, the silence of 'no commits' echoing louder than any interview rejection. You have the technical skills, but recruiters don't see code; they see a lack of proof. The gap between a student project and a hireable asset is the ability to ship complex, revenue-driving features that actually matter to a business.

Today, we bridge that gap. We aren't building another To-Do list. We are engineering a robust SaaS pricing architecture. By mastering the intersection of business logic and clean code using Antigravity and Claude Code, you move from 'student' to 'architect'. This is how you build the proof that gets you hired.

steps.title: Define Multi-Tiered Logic
steps.description: Establish the data structure for Free, Pro, and Enterprise tiers. This forces you to handle edge cases in user permissions and feature gating.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a TypeScript schema for a SaaS pricing model with three tiers. Include logic for feature flags based on the user's selected plan.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'Generate the pricing schema and feature flag middleware'

steps.title: Integrate Stripe Subscription Flow
steps.description: Connect your backend to Stripe to handle checkout sessions. This is the gold standard for proving you can manage sensitive financial data.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a Node.js controller for creating a Stripe checkout session. Ensure it handles success and cancel URLs correctly.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'Implement stripe checkout session creation in the backend'

steps.title: Implement Webhook Event Handlers
steps.description: Handle asynchronous events from Stripe to update user subscription status in your database. This teaches you event-driven architecture.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Draft an Express.js webhook listener for Stripe to update user database records upon 'customer.subscription.updated' events.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'Create stripe webhook listener for subscription updates'

steps.title: Build Usage-Based Metering
steps.description: Implement a system that tracks user activity and triggers upgrades based on limits. This is a high-level SaaS engineering skill.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a service to track API usage per user and compare it against their plan limits. Return a 403 if they exceed their quota.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'Implement usage-based throttling and plan limit checks'

steps.title: Polish the Pricing Dashboard
steps.description: Build the frontend UI that allows users to toggle between monthly and yearly billing. UX is what separates good apps from great ones.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a React component for a pricing table with a toggle for monthly/yearly billing. Use Tailwind CSS for a modern look.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'Build the pricing toggle UI component'

pro_tips: Always store your pricing configuration in a separate JSON or config file, not hardcoded in your controllers.
Implement 'Stripe Test Mode' effectively to demonstrate you understand how to separate dev/prod environments.
Use TypeScript interfaces for your pricing models to prevent runtime errors in your billing logic.
Document your API endpoints with Swagger/OpenAPI; recruiters love seeing professional documentation in your repo.
Include a README that explains the 'Why' behind your architecture choices, not just the 'How'.
content.next_steps: Deploy your app to Vercel or Railway, add a detailed case study to your personal website, and link it directly on your resume under a 'Featured Projects' section.
content.conversion_block: You now have the technical blueprint to build a professional-grade SaaS pricing engine that sets you apart from every other applicant. Don't just learn—ship. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.

Handle asynchronous events from Stripe to update user subscription status in your database. This teaches you event-driven architecture.
Implement a system that tracks user activity and triggers upgrades based on limits. This is a high-level SaaS engineering skill.
